name: Roy Brooks
abstract: Roy Brooks (March 9, 1938 – November 15, 2005) was an American jazz drummer.

concerts.label: The Charles Mingus Quintet featuring Dizzy Gillespie, Trumpet

about_quotes.speaker: Curtis Fuller
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Brooks. >> [INTERVIEWEE] Brooks, then the other guy from Tennessee. ... [INTERVIEWER] Roy Brooks? [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah. ... [INTERVIEWER] One Time I saw Brooks ... [INTERVIEWEE] He was out there! You know where he is now, or where he was?
about_quotes.doc_title: Curtis Fuller — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Burrell
about_quotes.evidence: some people who really were there a lot and played were Donald Byrd and Barry Harris and Sonny Red and Elvin and Roy Brooks
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Burrell — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Wendell Harrison
about_quotes.evidence: Lonnie Hillyer, Roy Brooks and those guys was in high school — they was like two years ahead of me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Wendell Harrison interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 10, 2022 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Mcbee
about_quotes.evidence: So I entered the world of Paul Chambers, of Ron Carter, of Roy Brooks, of you name it.
